item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's all Creative Commons here, so you can use it, just need to attribute it (SCP wiki has a page on Licensing)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: hope you don't mind but I hope to use 3293 in one of my articles (currently thinking tale). Basically I want to use this tragedy as an example of how the Foundation's cold methods can sometimes lead to disaster, and how a more empathetic approach can help prevent said disasters (sorry if I'm not being clear)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, that took an unexpectedly Akira turn.

Something I really appreciate: There's no sanctimonious, pontificating "we fulfilled our mission" researcher's note at the end, as there often is in the more overtly tragic items. The awfulness of it is just left there, stark and cold.

The takeaway from this I get is that someone at the Foundation is too sorry to lecture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An effective piece with just enough simmer of sentimentality and heartache without overcooking it.

Nitpicks
– hide block

Eventually, SCP-3293 grew withdrawn and hostile towards Foundation staff, and had to be sedated for feedings and sleep, among others.

Among what others? The sentence is not grammatically correct and should have a preposition of some kind for clarity.

As previous containment breach indicates…

Should it be “breaches indicate”?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-3293: Do you know Mr. Guy? If he give it back I will you a cookie when I get it back. Mr. Guy can have one too.

i think this is a typo

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well paced and very uncomfortable to read. I'm pleased to see child dialogue that doesn't fall into the usual tropes of poor pronounciation and missing articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It seems -22, the last tool he made, was to override amnestics? So basically mnestics but mechanical instead of chemical?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It seems -22, the last tool he made, was to override amnestics? So basically mnestics but mechanical instead of chemical?

Mistake. Delete? Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It seems this kid might be a plain reality bender whose powers manifest the way they do either due to personality type or self-identity, meaning he might be able to do much more, but this is what he thinks he's capable of, or is better at realizing technology vs other things like psychic powers.

I wonder if every reality bender is like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Until such that that SCP-3293 can be transported to a high-security Keter-class…

I'm pretty sure you meant "Until such time that…"

Other than that, +1. Having super-powers did not turn out well for this kid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like the last device backfired because the only thing the kid wanted was to go home. Everything else had a solid idea behind it (a teleporter, a dig-through-anything shovel, stuff like that), but the kid was so rushed and frustrated that he didn't have any more ideas. He just wanted to be home.

I'm so very sad. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fortunately for the Foundation, this Calvin had no Hobbes to rescue him.

The idea of the child being placed in what amounts to sensory deprivation solitary confinement was painful. The climax was worse; my headcanon is that he was trying to transform into something that could 'go home' - to parents who would not remember and could not recognize him.

Secure. Contain. Protect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Painfully delightful, I especially loved the language the kid used for his own machines. Really sells the idea that he's just playing pretend, until the game stops being fun to play.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This SCP makes me sad, it feels like the Foundation really dropped the ball, with this kid. T_T Like maybe if they had contained his parents as well, they could have avoided a lot of these escape attempts and kept the kid stable and happier. Of course this all is obvious in hindsight, maybe the Foundation will do better next time, dammit first 239 and now 3293. Stop killing kids, or driving kids to kill themselves Foundation! >~<

Well written overall +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually remember a very similar SCP you made like a year or so ago before you deleted it. Glad to see it up again in some fashion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"SCP-3293: Do you know Mr. Guy? If he give it back I will you a cookie when I get it back. Mr. Guy can have one too."
I don't know if this is a mistake or intentional but I think this is supposed to say will give you a cookie?
Heartbreaking story though, loved the interview. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lord I'm a broken person inside here huh?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely heartbreaking. The interview reflects a real child, especially with the "Um's". The neutralization is horrid. And the last confiscated item… Damn. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Due to a history of non-compliance and the nature of its anomaly, SCP-3293 has not been given any standard amnesties.

I assume this was meant to be "amenities" but your brain defaulted to amnestics?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That ending was painful, great job.

item.title: Re: Turn on those mokey-trols
item.content:encoded: Neato, I always love adding in references to real-life people I know. And the story is super heart-wrenching as well.

item.title: Turn on those mokey-trols
item.content:encoded:
This is about a friend of mine from grade school who said he was building a death star in his closet. This one's for you, Michael.

The picture is mine. It's one of my younger brothers. Don't tell him; he's already appeared in at least one other article.

I think Joreth wrote part of this? Somebody edited my old sandbox to add some text but the edit history didn't carry over when I moved it to the new one, so I'm not sure. I'm going to preemptively add him as a co-author but I'll remove it if I'm wrong.

Anyway, I hope you all enjoy this one.
